item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, I know this has been around for a while but,
“this locker must be large enough to properly house no fewer than five human subjects at any given time…”
I feel like that requirement would exceed in size what we consider to be a “locker”.
Anyway I liked this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the fact that you managed to successfully pull off a time-traveling Lime.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired - edit implemented.
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Proposed Edits
- Proposed Edits

The crosslink proposed is to link the article to the Site-81 Dossier page. The article mentions Site-81 in its containment procedures, while the Dossier page links to the article as an object within containment.

Editing this:

SCP-2540 is to be contained within an anomalous object storage locker at Site-81, outfitted with Plexiglas paneling in place of the standard steel alloy.

To this:

SCP-2540 is to be contained within an anomalous object storage locker at Site-81, outfitted with Plexiglas paneling in place of the standard steel alloy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On rereading, I gotta wonder if this has anything to do with that house that steals Foundation personnel so they can water the plants.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All security personnel, and the swordfish, escaped with no injuries.

Andddddd upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let's be honest
You made it into a lime because it rhymes with time.

Of course, that's not a crime

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminded me a bit of the simultaneously omniscient and stupid raspberry, but I enjoyed this.
"All security personnel, and the swordfish, escaped with no injuries."
+1 and the password is always swordfish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I realize this is a bit of an old post, but I like explaining things.

The best way to understand how 4D space looks, or rather, why it DOES look the way it does, is to look at 2D space.

First, imagine there is a 2-dimensional being. It exists in height and width, without depth. Because it lacks depth, it technically has no mass and can freely pass through 3-dimensional objects without affecting them. Conversely, we can't perceive them, because their lack of depth means they can't make sound, can't be touched, and light doesn't reflect or scatter off of them. Say for a minute though that one of these beings encountered us. Because they can't perceive depth, they couldn't see all of us. Instead, they would only see cross sections of us as they passed through us.

Now scale that up to three and four dimensions, with the fourth dimension being time. You have a four-dimensional form, which is a long snake-like thing stretching from before you were born to after you die, the entirety of your existence, and you experience it in three dimensional cross sections, each slightly different from those around it. The idea behind time travel is essentially exiting the snake you're in and then re-entering it in a different spot after travelling through 4D space.

Here's another way. Say there is a 2D world on the floor, and you're walking over it. 2D beings would be completely unaware of your existence until your foot comes down, and then they would see the imprint you leave on their world. Now imagine a 4D being is travelling along, periodically intersecting with our "snake". It would look to us as though they appear, then vanish, then reappear at a later time, because they only briefly cross our 4D form, like you only briefly connect with the 2D floor world.

The idea behind time travel is that you move through 4D space to intersect with a different part of the snake.

In this article's case, it seems the four dimensional object is simply, or at least in the shape of, the four-dimensional form of a lime, which is perforated. As it rotates in 4D space, it crosses the various 4D snakes, scooping out 3D cross sections of them and depositing them elsewhere along the line. The message appears to be the owner saying that he has to leave it unattended for a while and apologizing for any inconvenience it causes us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I saw banana, I immediately thought about the guy in the future being at a place that had banana trees.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really can't understand how a 3-dimensional cross-section of a 4-dimensional cylinder can have a form of a lime. I suppose it could be a sphere. Anyway the picture of a lime inside of a hypercube is adorable, and the article in whole is very interesting. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "requiring manual observation at all times." This bit gave me trouble, since it sounds like you intend for a person to touch SCP-2540 with their hands. How about "direct visual observation"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The one thing I don't fully get is: why the approximate translation notes like "fleshy/squishy" if the note was written in crude English?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I prefer spatial anomolemons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (Is there a way to delete all my comments?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2540 is a localized temporal anomaly that can only be perceived as a Citrus aurantiifolia, or key lime.

I was pleasantly surprised that I called this as a Kaktus article at this point.

Potentially it will be transferred to a different/banana? location

Aaand upvoted. The best and most important line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
2. Symbol was accompanied by what looked to be a drawing of a vaguely octopoid creature bearing a large grin.

Ha, interdenominational smiley faces are the best

loved the scp. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Larry Niven wrote a short story, "Rotating Cylinders and the Possibility of Global Causality Violation", that borrowed its title from Tipler's paper =D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it!

It's very different/banana.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this article from the moment I started reading it, but when I saw this:

"All security personnel, and the swordfish, escaped with no injuries."

I started smiling. What happened at the end made me smile even more.

One minor nitpick in Incident Log 2 though: it reads "The security personnel reported feeling a similar to those in Log 2540-1." at the time of this writing. I am figuring it should read "a feeling" instead?

Regardless of the small grammar issue, I really like this article, as it brightened up my day. You have my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tried understanding how something should be able to be 4- dimensional… I failed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this so much. So much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Words fail me. +1

And now I want key lime pie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Illegible is probably a better word, I'll make that edit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unless they accidentally slipped in a little of their native language.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another minor nitpick:

[UNTRANSLATABLE]

You mean indecipherable or possibly illegible? I mean, considering it's very poor English you wouldn't be translating again technically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+very minor nitpick
– hide block

Citrus aurantiifolia is a tree, not a fruit. What you have here is a fruit from a member of C. aurantiifolia.

item.title: Lime?
item.content:encoded:
So, what did the lime thing had to do with the Tipler Cylinder thing? I see that the Russian guy had contacted with some lime, but I am not exactly understanding this…..

And why a swordfish? Or the surprising feeling? The missing legs? Stone tablets? Bananas and aliens?

Anyway, in the midst of confusion, I upvoted still because I just liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found this was way over my head. Couldn't follow it enough to read the whole thing. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was really skeptical after seeing that diagram, but the article pulled off the wtf factor well enough for an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Luv u tho bb

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are my new favorite writerbot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upboated so hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait wait wait wait wait
Is this insinuating that all universes are connected by a bunch of sentient fruits
Oh my God yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
YOU'RE LATE
YOU'rE LaTe

mine's not even done
qq
upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not a fan of the end of it with the whole observed by whatever entity thing, but ultimately, the well done first part and the following sentence

All security personnel, and the swordfish, escaped with no injuries.

led me to "upgrade" to neutral vote.

item.title: Downvote for lack of Special Coconut Procedures
item.content:encoded:
>:( I told you dawg

No but seriously, I actually find myself liking this. I was pretty neutral when I read an early version, but expanding it beyond the interview log (which was also changed, probably for the better) gives it enough charm for me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I am a little disappointed that the time lime was not contained with in a coconut (and potentially mixed all up) this is still a very entertaining article. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A Tipler Cylinder perceived by humans as a lime. Fantastic! An upvote for you, my squishy friend creature.

item.title: Actually "space and time lime", but...
item.content:encoded:
Ok so this is the result of a challenge between myself and Paroxysm, where we each would give the other a concept and see what we can do with them. The one he gave me was of his first article, a lime what is also a Tipler Cylinder.

If you're not sure what a Tipler Cylinder is, I don't blame you! Neither did I. So here's the Wikipedia link. Hope it helps!

Once again, so much thanks for everybody who looked at this and had an opinion, notably Athena_Grey, TwistedGears, CryogenChaos, and Paroxysm.

As far as the duel is concerned, I'm going to consider it null and void, since neither of us posted our articles by the deadline. Still, this was fun to write, and I hope you enjoy it! Your comments, critiques, and criticisms are always welcome :)

Edit: The main image is from here: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Dimension_levels.svg?uselang=ru and the little insert of a lime is my own, released under Creative Commons.
